refnamediv.refpurpose: Returns the next vector in the sequence.

listitem.para: The matrix containing the next vector in the sequence. The matrix has a 1 x s, where s is the dimension of the space.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: The generated vector is computed depending on the generator associated with the -method option. The current object is both an input argument and an output argument, since internal settings are updated during the process.
The internal data is definitely updated into a new state which cannot be reversed and the produced vector should be stored for later use if required.

When an object is created this setting is initialized to 0. Whenever the randonumber_next method is called, the sequence index is updated and incremented with 1. If this option is passed to the lowdisc_cget method, it allows to know the number of vectors which have allready been generated, i.e. the number of calls to lowdisc_next.

By default, the sequence produced by the pseudo-random number generators is allways the same from session to session. This is because the generators uses a seed which is allways the same. This behaviour may be changed by setting a specific seed value with the -seed option or may be changed by setting the -seedmethod option.

By default, the sequence produced by the low discrepancy generators is allways the same from session to session. This behaviour can be changed by setting a positive integer value to the -sequenceindex option. This allows to modify the starting point of the low discrepancy sequence.

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.para: In the following example, one uses the Halton sequence in dimension 2. The first element in the sequence is the vector [1.0/2.0 1.0/3.0].
